home.title: Style is a system, not a guess.
home.description: Fashion Area is not a trend feed. It is a system for making structured decisions about clothing: what to buy, what to wear, and why one outfit works while another does not.

entryPoints.system: The four dimensions of every outfit.
entryPoints.guides: Practical articles for building a wardrobe.
entryPoints.outfits: Worked examples assembled from the system.
entryPoints.clothing: How to evaluate and buy individual pieces.
entryPoints.psychology: Why clothing changes how you are perceived.
entryPoints.shop: Curated picks that survive the system.

home.ctaTitle: Build your wardrobe on a system.
home.ctaDescription: The Method is a 3-step process for making consistent style decisions: no taste required.

cookie.bannerBody: We use necessary cookies to run the site. Analytics and marketing cookies load only after your explicit consent.
cookie.modalBody: Choose which optional cookies Fashion Area may use. You can change this later from the footer.
cookie.necessaryBody: Required for security, page delivery, and consent storage. Always active.
cookie.analyticsBody: Helps us understand aggregate site usage. Google Analytics loads only when enabled.
cookie.marketingBody: Reserved for advertising and retargeting pixels. No marketing scripts load unless enabled.

legal.privacyDescription: How HELPERG LLC processes personal data for Fashion Area.
legal.cookieDescription: How Fashion Area uses necessary, analytics, and marketing cookies.

home.title: The Style Operating System
home.description: A system for thinking about clothing, building wardrobes, and making better style decisions.

method.title: The Method
method.description: How we approach style: a clear, repeatable method for building a wardrobe.

system.title: The System
system.description: The vocabulary behind every wardrobe decision: formality, silhouette, color, and layer.

guides.title: Guides
guides.description: Practical articles on building a wardrobe that works.

outfits.title: Outfits
outfits.description: Worked examples of outfits assembled using the system.

clothing.title: Clothing
clothing.description: How to evaluate clothing: fabric, construction, fit, and buying decisions.

psychology.title: Psychology
psychology.description: How clothing changes perception: yours and other people's.

shop.title: Shop
shop.description: Curated clothing picks that survive the system. Coming soon.

the-formality-ladder-explained.title: The Formality Ladder Explained
the-formality-ladder-explained.description: A 7-level system for placing any garment in social context, plus the ±1 rule that determines whether an outfit holds together.

capsule-wardrobe-for-men.title: Capsule Wardrobe for Men
capsule-wardrobe-for-men.description: Twelve pieces that solve every weekday outfit problem and the rule that decides whether a thirteenth belongs.

how-to-dress-better.title: How to Dress Better
how-to-dress-better.description: Three constraints that improve any outfit immediately. None of them require buying anything new.

simple-outfit-formulas.title: Simple Outfit Formulas
simple-outfit-formulas.description: The four formulas that cover 80% of real-world dressing and how to vary them without breaking the system.

how-to-check-clothing-quality.title: How to Check Clothing Quality
how-to-check-clothing-quality.description: Five tests you can run in 90 seconds before buying anything. Most failures are visible if you know where to look.

clothing-and-first-impressions.title: Clothing and First Impressions
clothing-and-first-impressions.description: How people read your clothes in the first six seconds and the three signals that move the read most.

why-minimal-style-works.title: Why Minimal Style Works
why-minimal-style-works.description: Minimalism is a constraint, not an aesthetic. Here is what the constraint actually buys you.
